question.text: A commodity exchange guarantees all trades routed through its members. This guarantee remains even in case of:

answer.text: Default, insolvency, or non-performance of any client or member
Only trades below Rs 1 crore value
Only hedging trades, not speculative trades

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The exchange guarantees all trades through its members, even in case of default, insolvency, or non-performance of any client or member — this is the settlement guarantee. **The Trap:** The guarantee is not limited to minor delays, small trades, or hedging trades. It covers ALL trades regardless of size, participant type, or purpose. **Cross-Exam Context:** Settlement guarantee is a fundamental feature covered in Series VII and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This guarantee is backed by the Settlement Guarantee Fund (SGF) maintained by the clearing corporation — one of the strongest risk mitigation features of exchange-traded derivatives.
